title: monardella villosa subsp. neglecta
intro: Monardella villosa subsp. neglecta, also known as Foothill Mountain Monardella or Foothill Coyote Mint, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family that is native to the southwestern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to its essential oils containing compounds like carvacrol and limonene. The health benefits of Monardella villosa subsp. neglecta include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues.
